troubleshooting.solution: PETG is highly hygroscopic and absorbs moisture from the air, which causes print oozing and whispy strings. To fix: 1) Dry your PETG filament in a dryer at 65°C for 6-8 hours. 2) Increase retraction length to 1.4mm (for direct drive) and retraction speed to 45mm/s. 3) Reduce printing temperature by 5-10°C (down to 240°C if prints permit). 4) Enable Z-hop only when crossing walls.

If PLA prints lift at the corners or fail to adhere: 1) Clean the build plate thoroughly with warm water and dish soap (Dawn), followed by 99% IPA to remove oils. 2) Set bed temperature to 55-65°C for the first layer. 3) Disable cooling fans for the first 3 layers. 4) Use a Brim (5-10mm width) in slicer settings. 5) Check bed leveling and Z-offset calibration.

ABS and ASA shrink significantly upon cooling, leading to layer cracking and peeling. 1) Always print with a closed chamber door and lid. 2) Set bed temperature to 90-110°C. 3) Preheat the chamber for 15-20 minutes before starting the print. 4) Set chamber cooling fan to 0%. 5) Keep auxiliary fan completely turned off.

When the nozzle is clogged: 1) Perform a 'cold pull' (heat to 220°C, push filament, let cool to 90°C, and pull out firmly). 2) Use a 0.4mm cleaning needle to clear debris from the nozzle tip while heated to 250°C. 3) Check extruder gear tension. 4) Ensure filament path has no sharp bends.

faq.question: What is the maximum nozzle temperature of the Bambu Lab X1C?
faq.answer: The maximum nozzle temperature of the Bambu Lab X1C is 300°C. The software and safety guardrails restrict heating beyond this to prevent damage to the hotend.

faq.question: What is the maximum bed temperature of the Bambu Lab X1C?
faq.answer: The maximum bed temperature of the Bambu Lab X1C is 120°C (depending on the voltage configuration, typical limit is 110-120°C).

faq.question: Which build plates are supported on the Bambu Lab X1C?
faq.answer: Supported plates include: Cool Plate, Engineering Plate, High Temperature Plate, Textured PEI Plate, and Smooth PEI Plate.
